name: EB-1
tagline: The fastest employment-based green card — no PERM labor certification required
description: The EB-1 is the first preference employment-based immigrant visa category, reserved for individuals with extraordinary ability (EB-1A), outstanding professors and researchers (EB-1B), and multinational managers and executives (EB-1C). It is the fastest path to a green card for most nationalities because it does not require PERM labor certification.

requirements: EB-1A: Demonstrate extraordinary ability in sciences, arts, education, business, or athletics through sustained national/international acclaim. Can self-petition.
EB-1B: Outstanding professor or researcher with international recognition and a job offer from a university or private employer. Requires employer petition.
EB-1C: Multinational manager or executive who has worked for the petitioning company for 1 of the last 3 years. Requires employer petition.
All categories: no PERM labor certification required (unlike EB-2 and EB-3)
benefits: No PERM labor certification — much faster than EB-2 or EB-3
EB-1A allows self-petition — no employer required
Priority dates are current for most nationalities (except India and China backlogs)
Leads directly to lawful permanent residence (green card)
Spouse and children under 21 receive green cards as dependents
EB-1A uses same standard as O-1A visa — strong overlap in evidence

steps.title: Evidence preparation (EB-1A/B)
steps.description: Gather extraordinary ability or outstanding researcher evidence: awards, publications, citations, high salary, critical roles, media coverage.

steps.title: File I-140 petition
steps.description: File Form I-140 Immigrant Petition with USCIS. Premium processing available for 15-business-day decision.

steps.title: Adjustment of status or consular processing
steps.description: If in the U.S. and priority date is current, file I-485 adjustment of status concurrently or after I-140 approval. If abroad, consular processing.

processing.timeline: I-140: 4–6 months standard, 15 business days with premium. Adjustment of status: 8–24 months after I-140 approval (if priority date current)
processing.cost: Government fees: $715 (I-140) + $1,440 (I-485) + filing fee. Premium processing for I-140: $2,805. Attorney fees additional.
documents: For EB-1A: Evidence meeting at least 3 of the USCIS criteria for extraordinary ability
For EB-1B: Offer letter from university or qualifying private employer, evidence of international recognition
For EB-1C: Corporate ownership documentation, job description, evidence of managerial/executive role
Personal documents: passport, birth certificate, marriage certificate (if applicable)
Medical exam (I-693) for adjustment of status

faqs.question: What is the difference between EB-1A and EB-1B?
faqs.answer: EB-1A is for individuals with extraordinary ability in any field — sciences, arts, education, business, or athletics. It requires no employer and no PERM. You must demonstrate sustained national or international acclaim meeting at least 3 of 10 USCIS criteria. EB-1B is specifically for outstanding professors and researchers and requires an offer of a tenured or tenure-track teaching position or a comparable research position, plus international recognition in the academic field.

faqs.question: Can I self-petition for EB-1A?
faqs.answer: Yes. EB-1A is unique in that it allows self-petition — you do not need a U.S. employer to sponsor you. You can file Form I-140 on your own behalf, demonstrating that you intend to continue working in your area of extraordinary ability. This makes EB-1A ideal for entrepreneurs, independent researchers, artists, and athletes who do not have a traditional employer.

faqs.question: How does EB-1C relate to L-1A?
faqs.answer: EB-1C is designed for multinational managers and executives — the same category as L-1A nonimmigrant status. If you have been in the U.S. on L-1A status and your company wants to sponsor you for a green card, EB-1C is typically the fastest pathway. It does not require PERM labor certification and uses the same qualifying relationship standards as L-1.

faqs.question: What is the EB-1 priority date situation for Indian and Chinese nationals?
faqs.answer: Unlike most other nationalities, Indian and Chinese nationals face significant backlogs in the EB-1 category due to per-country caps. The wait for Indian nationals especially can be several years even in EB-1. We help clients understand their current priority date situation and evaluate whether simultaneous EB-2 NIW filings make strategic sense.

faqs.question: Is EB-1A harder to get than O-1A?
faqs.answer: EB-1A and O-1A use very similar evidentiary standards, but EB-1A is generally considered slightly more demanding because it leads to permanent residence rather than a temporary visa. A successful O-1A is a very positive signal for an EB-1A case, but USCIS adjudicators may apply additional scrutiny. We help clients build the strongest possible record for both categories simultaneously.
